assertions.text: The truck left the road (departed from the roadway).
The truck struck a traffic signal pole (impact with a pole).
There was a single impact in the crash (one distinct collision).
After the impact, the truck came to rest (final state was stationary following the collision).
The single impact with the traffic signal pole was the only collision-related event (no additional collisions or secondary impacts occurred).

verdicts.evidence: The claim that the truck left the road cannot be judged from the available telemetry channels: there is no GPS position or lateral displacement channel in the window info or statistics, so departure-from-road is unverifiable from sensors. For example, the get_window_info channels list lacks position/GPS (T1) and compute_stats shows only speed and delta-V (T2).

Impact with a traffic signal pole (a pole) is a scene/object-level statement not recorded by vehicle sensors. The telemetry shows a crash pulse with longitudinal [7.3 km/h final](T2) and lateral [4.1 km/h final](T1) delta-V values but does not identify the struck object, so the pole impact is unverifiable from sensors. See delta-V magnitudes in compute_stats (T2) and window info summary (T1).

The narrative asserts a single impact, but the EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 3](T1) and detect_events found multiple impact-related detections including two impact_spike detections (at [t=-5.0 s and t=-1.5 s](T3)) and crash-pulse delta-V detections for both longitudinal [7.7 km/h at t=0.251 s](T3) and lateral [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T3). The presence of multiple impact spike detections and the EDR 'num_events_recorded' value contradicts the claim of a single impact.

The claim that the truck came to rest after the impact is supported by the speed series which decreases from an initial [156.2 km/h at t=-5.0 s] to [122.4 km/h at t=0.0 s] (T2) and the crash-pulse final delta-Vs are modest with longitudinal final [7.3 km/h](T2), indicating the post-impact vehicle speed would be reduced; additionally, the brake channel shows a transition to on at [t=-0.5 s] and the EDR window ends at t=0.25 s with no further motion data, consistent with the vehicle coming to rest after the collision per available sensors (brake transition at -0.5 s shown in compute_stats (T2) and impact delta-V final value (T2)).

The assertion that the single impact was the only collision-related event is contradicted by sensor evidence: the EDR 'num_events_recorded' is [3](T1) and detect_events reports multiple impact-related detections including two impact_spike events (peaks at [t=-5.0 s and t=-1.5 s](T3)) and crash-pulse delta-V maxima for longitudinal [7.7 km/h at t=0.251 s](T3) and lateral [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T3), indicating additional collision-related events occurred.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows multiple impact-related detections and EDR 'num_events_recorded' [3](T1), contradicting the single-impact assertions. Object-level details like leaving the road or striking a pole are not present in the sensor channels and are therefore unverifiable (T1, T2).

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
